meta.title: 4 Days Jakarta Bandung + White Crater Volcano | From RM1288 | Dhesu Travel
meta.description: Experience Bandung's Kawah Putih White Crater volcano, factory outlet shopping, Rengganis Suspension Bridge, Taman Mini Indonesia, and Monas Jakarta in 4 days. Private tour from RM1288. Book with Dhesu Travel.
meta.keywords: Bandung Jakarta tour Malaysia, White Crater Bandung, Kawah Putih volcano, Jakarta Bandung holiday, Riau factory outlet Bandung, Indonesia tour package from KL, Monas Jakarta tour

hero.title: 4 Days Jakarta Bandung
hero.intro: Striking volcanic landscapes, world-famous factory outlets, Dutch colonial heritage, and Indonesia's national icons — this 4-day private tour connects Bandung and Jakarta in the most satisfying way, combining natural wonders with urban exploration.
hero.heroImageAlt: Kawah Putih White Crater volcanic lake with turquoise sulphuric water in Bandung

includes: Hotel accommodation (3 nights: 2 nights Bandung, 1 night Jakarta)
All meals as specified (Day 1: Lunch & Dinner; Days 2 & 3: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner; Day 4: Breakfast)

overview.heading: Two Cities, One Volcano, Endless Shopping
body: Bandung and Jakarta are Indonesia's most dynamic city pair — the former a cool highland fashion capital famous for factory outlets, creative cuisine, and stunning volcanic scenery; the latter a vast megalopolis with world-class museums, monuments, and cultural attractions. This 4-day private tour combines the best of both: start in Bandung with factory outlet shopping and a visit to the ethereal Kawah Putih White Crater volcano, then move to Jakarta for cultural highlights including Taman Mini Indonesia Indah and the iconic National Monument.

The itinerary is designed for Malaysian travellers who want to cover the Jakarta-Bandung corridor efficiently without sacrificing depth. All transport between cities, meals, entrance fees, and your personal guide are included. Group discounts make this an excellent choice for family groups — 3 adults save RM200/person, and 5 adults save RM300/person compared to the standard rate.

items.title: Kawah Putih White Crater Volcano
items.body: Kawah Putih — literally 'White Crater' — is one of Java's most photogenic natural wonders: a turquoise-white sulphuric crater lake formed by an 11th-century eruption of Mount Patuha, surrounded by bleached rock and wisps of volcanic gas. The colour shifts throughout the day from jade green to milky white depending on sulphur concentration.
items.imageAlt: Turquoise sulphuric crater lake of Kawah Putih White Crater Bandung

items.title: Rengganis Suspension Bridge
items.body: Strung across a highland valley near the White Crater area, the Rengganis Suspension Bridge offers dramatic views over Bandung's volcanic highlands and surrounding tea plantations. It is one of West Java's most popular new attractions and a thrilling walk for those comfortable with heights.
items.imageAlt: Rengganis suspension bridge over Bandung highland valley near Kawah Putih

items.title: Bandung Factory Outlets & Shopping
items.body: Bandung is Malaysia's shopping twin — clothing, embroidery, leather goods, and accessories at factory outlet prices. The Riau area outlets are the most famous, offering branded and locally made fashion at a fraction of retail prices. Pasar Baru's eight-storey building offers garments, textiles, and footwear from every region of Indonesia.
items.imageAlt: Shoppers browsing Bandung factory outlet fashion district

items.title: Jakarta's National Icons
items.body: Taman Mini Indonesia Indah showcases the entire Indonesian archipelago in miniature — traditional houses, cultural performances, and exhibitions from Aceh to Papua. The National Monument (Monas), a 132-metre gold-tipped obelisk in the centre of Merdeka Square, is Jakarta's most recognisable landmark and symbol of independence.
items.imageAlt: National Monument Monas obelisk in Jakarta's Merdeka Square

days.title: Arrive Jakarta > Bandung > Riau Factory Outlets
days.description: Your guide meets you at Soekarno-Hatta International Airport, Jakarta. Transfer to Bandung (approximately 3 hours via the Cipularang Toll Road or Puncak scenic route), stopping for lunch at a local restaurant en route. Arrive in Bandung — West Java's cool highland capital at 768 metres elevation — and head directly to the famous Riau factory outlet district. Browse multiple outlet stores offering fashion, embroidery, branded clothing, bags, and accessories at factory prices — a long-standing favourite for Malaysian shoppers visiting West Java. Return to the hotel for dinner and rest.
suggestedActivities: Browse fashion and embroidery at Riau outlet district
Shop for Bandung batik and local clothing
Explore Bandung's vibrant food street near the hotel

days.title: Cibaduyut Leather > White Crater Volcano > Rengganis Bridge
days.description: After hotel breakfast, head to Cibaduyut — Bandung's legendary leather goods district, where dozens of family-run workshops and showrooms line the streets, selling shoes, bags, wallets, belts, and jackets at prices well below retail. Browse at leisure and negotiate for the best deals. Lunch at a local restaurant, then continue south into the volcanic highlands to Kawah Putih (White Crater) — one of Java's most extraordinary natural sights. The turquoise-white sulphuric crater lake of Mount Patuha sits at 2,430 metres elevation, framed by bleached volcanic rock and drifting gas. The shifting colour of the water from jade green to milky white is mesmerising. Nearby, the Clubbing Lake Side area offers additional views over the crater complex, and the Rengganis Suspension Bridge — stretching across a highland valley — provides a thrilling walk with panoramic views of the surrounding hills and tea plantations. Dinner at a local restaurant and overnight in Bandung.
suggestedActivities: Shop for leather shoes and bags at Cibaduyut
Photograph Kawah Putih's colour-shifting crater lake
Walk Rengganis Suspension Bridge over the valley
Enjoy highland scenery and cool air

days.title: Pasar Baru Shopping > Jakarta > Taman Mini > Monas
days.description: Breakfast and hotel checkout, then head to Pasar Baru — Bandung's most famous eight-storey shopping complex offering garments, textiles, traditional fabrics, footwear, and handicrafts from across Indonesia at excellent prices. Stock up on batik, traditional weaving, and Indonesian fashion before transferring to Jakarta. Lunch at a local restaurant upon arrival in the capital. Afternoon cultural exploration begins at Taman Mini Indonesia Indah, a sprawling cultural park where life-sized traditional houses from all 34 Indonesian provinces are displayed alongside cultural museums, performances, and exhibitions — a fascinating window into Indonesia's extraordinary diversity. Continue to the National Monument (Monas), a 132-metre golden-tipped obelisk at the heart of Merdeka Square that has symbolised Indonesian independence since 1975. Dinner at a local Jakarta restaurant and overnight in the capital.
suggestedActivities: Last Bandung shopping at Pasar Baru textile centre
Explore Taman Mini Indonesia Indah cultural park
Photograph Monas National Monument at sunset
Dinner at a Jakarta street food hub

days.title: Jakarta Shopping > Departure
days.description: After hotel breakfast and checkout, enjoy a final morning of Jakarta shopping before your departure flight. Choose between Thamrin City Mall — a multi-level bazaar specialising in fashion, batik, and traditional crafts — or Mangga Dua Square, Jakarta's electronics and wholesale fashion hub. Both are excellent for last-minute gifts, clothing, and Indonesian souvenirs. Your guide transfers you to Soekarno-Hatta International Airport in time for your departure flight back to Malaysia. End of tour.
suggestedActivities: Shop for batik and crafts at Thamrin City Mall
Browse electronics at Mangga Dua Square
Last Indonesian street food before departure

whyBook.heading: Why Book Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Dhesu Travel has been organising Jakarta and Bandung private tours for Malaysian travellers since 1988. Our experienced West Java guides know exactly which factory outlets offer the best deals, the best time to visit Kawah Putih for the clearest water colour, and where to find authentic Sundanese cuisine off the tourist trail.

sections.heading: Bandung Jakarta Tour Package from Malaysia — 4 Days
sections.body: Jakarta and Bandung are among Malaysia's favourite Indonesia destinations — and for good reason. Bandung's factory outlets and volcanic scenery are a compelling combination, while Jakarta's cultural institutions and sprawling shopping malls offer a different urban experience. Flying from Kuala Lumpur to Jakarta (CGK) takes under 2 hours, and no visa is required for Malaysian passport holders. This 4-day private tour starting from RM1,288 per person covers all the major highlights — Kawah Putih White Crater, Riau factory outlets, Cibaduyut leather goods, Taman Mini, and Monas — with all meals and transport included.

sections.heading: Kawah Putih — Bandung's Most Spectacular Natural Attraction
sections.body: Kawah Putih (White Crater) is formed by the volcanic activity of Mount Patuha, an active stratovolcano in the Bandung highlands. The crater lake's extraordinary colour — shifting between turquoise, jade green, and milky white depending on sulphur concentration and time of day — makes it one of the most photogenic natural sites in all of Indonesia. At 2,430 metres elevation, the air is cool and crisp even at midday. The surrounding area also includes the Clubbing Lake Side viewpoint and the recently added Rengganis Suspension Bridge, making the White Crater area a half-day highland adventure in its own right.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Indonesia?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders can enter Indonesia visa-free for stays up to 30 days. Your passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates.

items.question: Which airport do I fly into for this tour?
items.answer: You fly into Soekarno-Hatta International Airport (CGK) in Jakarta. Direct flights from Kuala Lumpur take under 2 hours and are available on AirAsia, Malaysia Airlines, Batik Air, and Malindo Air. The tour begins in Jakarta and ends in Jakarta with airport drop-off on Day 4.

items.question: Is the White Crater safe to visit?
items.answer: Yes, Kawah Putih is generally safe for tourists but sulphur gas is present, especially on calm days. The local management provides masks at the entrance, and visits are typically limited to 30–45 minutes in the crater area. Your guide will advise on any current restrictions on the day of the visit.

items.question: How much budget should I allow for shopping in Bandung?
items.answer: Bandung factory outlets offer clothing from RM20–RM100 per piece, shoes from RM50–RM200, and leather goods from RM80 upwards. Most Malaysian visitors budget RM300–RM600 for personal shopping. The exchange rate for Indonesian Rupiah is favourable for Malaysians.

items.question: Can I upgrade to the WHOOSH bullet train experience?
items.answer: Yes — our 4 Days Jakarta Bandung by WHOOSH Bullet Train package (from RM1,388) replaces the standard road transfer from Bandung to Jakarta with a 34-minute high-speed train journey on Southeast Asia's first bullet train, reaching speeds of 350 km/h. The itinerary is otherwise very similar.
